enriched_points.expanded_content: 3D topological insulators demonstrate superior short-term device applications due to established materials, while 2D TIs, though promising, depend on less proven compounds.

Magnetic topological insulators like MnBi₂Te₄ exhibit unique electronic properties, including temperature-dependent resistance and distinct magnetic responses, enhancing potential for spintronic applications.
Higher-order TIs introduce novel edge states and unique phase transitions under varying temperature and magnetic fields, expanding their application in quantum computing and advanced materials.
